description: WolfHsmState::connected_client (lib.rs:88-92) calls .expect() if state.client is None, and every helper that uses it carries a // Panics: docstring saying 'call ensure_connected first'. This is a runtime-checked typestate. Idiomatic alternative: have ensure_connected return &mut Client (the connected reference) so callers cannot misuse the API. Sketch: fn connect_or_get<'a>(state: &'a mut WolfHsmState, server, client_id) -> Result<&'a mut Client, SecretError> — eliminates connected_client(), the .expect, and the panic docstrings. Filed as P3 elegance, not a bug — current code is correct, just not what a serious Rust reviewer expects to see.
title: connected_client() panic encodes a typestate via runtime expect; idiomatic Rust would use the type system
